findings.title: DIRECTIVE-013 PROP-073 sub-fixes shipped
findings.description: All 4 PROP-073 sub-fixes applied in one batched commit (15674330ad62cf3333a049ee68a1bd813dfd4636 on origin/main). (1) stage-delta gating prevents the mis-titled-commit failure mode (Step 3 pre-staged files mis-attributed under PROP-066 step4c sentinel title — forensic: c9e1d92 / 007d0fe / b7c36cb / fb52feb8 on 2026-05-31). (2) filename-ISO parse replaces the broken mtime-based SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE heuristic that false-positived because fresh-clone mtime reset matched all pre-existing sentinels. (3) write-path logging in writeSentinel emits absolute path so 'invoked-but-wrong-cwd' vs 'never invoked' are distinguishable. (4) AGENT_NOTES step4c-mis-titled bullet is detective visibility hook for any future regression of sub-fix #1's correctness.
findings.verification: grep -q 'PROP-073' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'ADDED_BY_4C' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'PROP-073' monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js && echo FIXED → FIXED
synthetic_tests_passed: stage-delta gating: with Step 3 pre-stage + no sentinel, PRE=1 POST=1 ADDED_BY_4C=0 → mis-titled commit correctly skipped
stage-delta gating: with sentinel written by script, PRE=1 POST=2 ADDED_BY_4C=1 → commits under correct title
filename-ISO parse: 3 pre-existing sentinels (09:07:58Z, 01:16:37Z, 21:28:55Z) — only 21:28:55Z (within 10min) counted; pre-existing correctly excluded (no false positives)
write-path logging: script run from /tmp/tinker-clone emitted '[PROP-066] wrote sentinel: /tmp/tinker-clone/monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-2026-05-31T21-28-55Z.json' as expected

findings.title: PROP-073 detective layer is active; underlying invocation reliability remains OPEN
findings.description: PROP-073 is detective scaffolding, not preventive — operator strategy shifted after 6 prior prevention iterations (PROP-066, 068, 072 hoist, 072 Layer 2, contextual reframe) all failed to make the LLM workspace-sync agent reliably invoke node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js. With PROP-073 deployed, the failure mode now manifests as the absence of any 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel: ...' commit (per sub-fix #1) instead of as silent mis-titled commits with lying contents. AGENT_NOTES 'step4c-mis-titled' (sub-fix #4) is a detective backstop for any sub-fix #1 regression. If 3 consecutive post-deploy cycles produce no actual sync-workspace-runs-*.json sentinel commits, operator will pursue option 2 tomorrow: architectural separation via new dome-step4c-sync scheduled agent whose single-action prompt structurally removes the pattern-matching abstraction failure.
canary_watch_points: next workspace-sync cycle (~top of next hour, 22:00Z): any 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel' commit MUST contain a real sentinel file (sub-fix #1 invariant)
AGENT_NOTES grep for 'step4c-mis-titled' literal in next ~5 cycles: presence confirms sub-fix #4 detective working; absence is normal (sub-fix #1 should prevent the mis-titled commits entirely)
stdout grep for '[PROP-066] wrote sentinel:' in next ~5 cycles: presence confirms script was actually invoked (the OPEN question — sub-fix #3 is the ground-truth signal)

after 3 consecutive cycles: if no real sync-workspace-runs-*.json sentinel landed via the 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel' commit path, the underlying invocation is still broken and PROP-074 (architectural separation) is the next move

findings.title: Root FS at 96% used at run start (443MB free)
findings.description: df -h / showed 9.6G total, 9.1G used, 443MB available at clone time. No stale /tmp/*-clone leftovers (only our active /tmp/tinker-clone at 290MB+). Per CLAUDE.md the sandbox baseline is ~8GB system image; the remaining ~1.6GB is project + transient. Within standing-empowerment safe range (project footprint warning ≥500MB, critical ≥1000MB; ours was the single active clone at ~290MB).
findings.no_action_reason: No leak; single active clone is the canonical /tmp/tinker-clone. The Cleanup step below removes it. Future tinker runs should observe this in their Mode 2 disk check as historical baseline.

next_tinker_run_priorities: Mode 2 / forward-watch: at next run, grep last ~3 workspace-sync cycles' commit titles for 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel: ' AND verify each contains a sync-workspace-runs-*.json file. Any mis-titled commit = sub-fix #1 regression (escalate immediately).

Mode 2 / forward-watch: grep last ~3 workspace-sync run-reports' AGENT_NOTES field for 'step4c-mis-titled' literal — presence indicates sub-fix #1 regression (impossible by construction unless prompt was reverted).

Mode 2 / forward-watch: grep last ~3 cycles' bash stdout (if captured) for '[PROP-066] wrote sentinel:' — presence rate = invocation rate of the helper script. Targets: ≥1/3 = invocation working; 0/3 = invocation still broken → escalate to option 2 (PROP-074 architectural separation).
Mode 3 if 3 cycles clean: continue normal cost-engineering work; PROP-073 closure becomes background.
Mode 4 if 0/3 invocation in next 3h: author PROP-074 (separate dome-step4c-sync agent) per the operator strategy.
